item.title: Explain this, humankind
item.description: Imagine this: a gift that’s not just a one-time gesture but a sustained act of care – a love letter service delivered monthly, complete with a poem, holographic sticker, and exclusive treasure. Gift it for up to a year – perfect for last-minute Christmas giving. No malls, no frantic scrolling, just handcrafted love mailed monthly. Make it happen: https://www.patreon.com/warandpeas/gift

item.title: The most successful animal you may never heard of
item.description:
No other animal has made up so much of the fauna on Earth as the genus Lystrosaurus once did, several million years before the first dinosaurs appeared
Despite appearances were Lystrosaurus not reptiles but therapsids. As therapsids they belonged to the same linage as, though were not themselves ancestors of, mammals (art by John Sibbick)
Various therapsid kindreds dominated the later part of the Permian period, 298.9 - 252.2 million years ago [MYA]. At the time were most landmasses joined into Pangea
Lystrosaurus species varied in size but are often compared to pigs. They had only two teeth but are presumed to had a horny beak to feed on plants with
They had a semi-sprawling gait and could wander far; and their strong forelimbs suggests they could dig for food as well as to nest in burrows
The Permian period ended with volcanism in Siberia for ca 2 million years, that caused Earth's most severe annihilation: the Permian–Triassic extinction event ended 83% of genera, 81% marine species and 70% of vertebrate species on land, as well as many insects and plants
Lystrosaurus lived across Pangea and fossils have been found in what is today India, Mongolia, China, European Russia, Antarctica and South Africa
The fossils were used as evidence by scientists in the 1960s for plate tectonics and that the continents once were connected
The now mostly empty world belonged to Lystrosaurus which prospered. Why these herbivores endured the Great Dying is debated; some suggest it was the abilities to dig and borrow, others to wander far, though some have called it simply luck
Yet no luck lasts forever: new competitors and predators evolved and about 248 MYA, between two and four million years after the Great Dying, the end came to Lystrosaurus. They left no descendants but a far less prosperous relative, Kannemeyeriiformes, diversified during the Triassic period. Its late descendants included Placerias (230 - 215 MYA) and Lisowicia (210 - 205 MYA), whence the reign of the dinosaurs began
item.dc:creator: Ngugi

item.title: Constructed from 1978 to 1982 in Noisy-le-Grand, a suburb located ten miles east of Paris, France, Les Espaces d'Abraxas is a notable housing estate designed by the late Catalan architect Ricardo Bofill, who passed away last year.

item.title: anybody else spending $10k for "health" insurance, on top of their premiums...?
item.description: ...besides the $143 per week I give the BCBS Hydra, this is what I'm paying "out-of-pocket" for my wife to live The BCBS Hydra has a 501 (c) (4) tax exempt status- put in place and maintained by your local and federal politicians... here is their public tax ID Number: 13-5656874 here's where you can view their 990 filing: https://apps.irs.gov/app/eos/ here's their 2022 filing, where they paid little to no taxes: https://apps.irs.gov/pub/epostcard/cor/135656874_202212_990O_2023120722082289.pdf -this is all public information- The BCBS Hydra is the only administer of Medicare in many states The BCBS Hydra takes in multi-Billion dollar "proceeds" every year A Propublica investigation found that Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Louisiana used guidelines from an outside company called AIM Specialty Health to reject appeals for denial, and that this practice was widespread The BCBS Hydra gave close to $10M to both political parties/candidates just this year... https://www.opensecrets.org/orgs/blue-cross-blue-shield/summary?all=2024&id=D000000109 elevanceHealth is the majority stakeholder in the BCBS/Anthem Hydra... with over $171BILLION in Sales alone... https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/ELEVANCE-HEALTH-INC-18740543/company/ I'll give you two guesses at who are the largest shareholders of elevanceHealth; and both will be right... Vanguard and BlackRock (private equity) BlackRock and Vanguard own over 15% of elevanceHealth which is the majority stakeholder in the BCBS Hydra BlackRock and Vanguard own literally everything... let me put into perspective, briefly... 3 People control two private companies that control everything you buy or do from the cradle to the grave, from the box of cereal you buy to the car you drive... ...and I I have to pay them money, or my wife dies I often think; am I complicit in all of this, is everyone complicit...? then I remember: we are in the dystopia and history will remember this time as a time of darkness let his be a voice to the future -we know we are not powerless, but we were afraid
item.dc:creator: ki4clz

item.title: New UnitedHealthcare CEO writes editorial in NYT and gets flooded with neg comments, which promptly get locked out
item.description: https://x.com/kenklippenstein/status/1867617063956582603 https://www.msn.com/en-us/news/us/ar-AA1vPue5 The New York Times turned off the comment section on the UnitedHealth CEO's op-ed after it became flooded with negative replies accusing the executive of empty promises. Andrew Witty, the CEO of UnitedHealth Group, mourned the loss of UnitedHeathCare CEO Brian Thompson in an editorial piece published by the New York Times Friday, while also offering sympathy to those frustrated with the healthcare system. But while Witty said that he was "willing to partner with anyone" to find ways to provide high-quality care at a lower cost, hundreds of readers mocked him, saying he offered no real solutions to the problem. Figures...
item.dc:creator: Castagere

item.title: The way things are currently is unacceptable.
item.description: In case you didn't see it on the front page or User Sub the other day, here is a link to a post I shared the other day with a super useful tip for dealing with insurance companies when they try to deny a claim by saying the treatment/medicine/etc "isn't medically necessary". There were a few commenters who expressed a disbelief that it actually works, but I was unable to update the description with clarification that I know from first hand experience that it DOES work. I've had to do it FOUR times this year, so I know from experience that it does in fact work as long as you have your information organized and ready to go right off the bat. https://imgur.com/gallery/share-this-if-you-live-us-could-save-you-loved-one-ton-of-pain-suffering-worse-Slq0LOL
item.dc:creator: Othagovna

Like what we do and how we do it? Leave a tip. Or don't. It's totally cool either way. Stay True & Stay Tuned - Meta Pathos

item.title: NO! THIS IS...Patrick?
item.description: TIL: Basket Stars belongs to the order of brittle stars, which are relatives of sea stars. They have five arms that branch into many smaller arms, forming a net-like shape. They use their arms to catch small animals that float in the water, such as crustaceans and jellyfish. They can live up to 35 years and weigh up to 5 kilograms. They are usually found in deep sea habitats or cold waters, but some species can be seen in shallow tropical reefs at night
item.dc:creator: LogicDude

item.title: Doug the xl bully is living the life
item.dc:creator: tampacl

item.title: You Gave Me All The Love You Had
item.description: I have left you now, but please don't be sad, You gave me all the love you had. You did so much for me in my time here, And I'll always hold those precious times near. I know someday you'll find love again, And into your life will come a new friend. Your heart will heal, though you'll never forget, Memories like the first time we met. Memories are wonderful, so keep them close, And remember all the good times the most. I am at peace now, so please don't be sad, You gave me all the love you had.
item.dc:creator: Orcinussalmonid
